level_signals: 5+ years of product management or research operations experience
Expected to own safety product roadmap independently
Requires credible engagement with safety researchers and technical depth

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of product management or research operations experience, with meaningful time working alongside research or ML teams at a technology or AI company.
must_haves.qualifier: with meaningful time working alongside research or ML teams at a technology or AI company

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Technical depth sufficient to engage credibly with safety researchers: you don't need to run evals yourself, but you need to understand what they mean and ask the right questions.
Genuine interest in AI safety and model behavior, including the real-world implications of deploying LLMs in enterprise contexts.
Comfortable operating in ambiguity — safety research surfaces unexpected findings, and this role requires good judgment about what to act on and how fast.
Able to work across researchers, engineers, and product teams and keep everyone aligned without flattening the nuance of what the research is actually saying.
Strong written communicator who can translate complex model behavior findings for non-technical audiences and knows when something needs to be escalated urgently.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Hands-on experience with LLM evaluation, red-teaming, safety benchmarking, or behavioral research.
Familiarity with AI-specific threat vectors: prompt injection, jailbreaks, RAG poisoning, or misuse patterns in agentic systems.
Background in trust and safety, content policy, or a research-adjacent operational role at a technology company.
Experience building zero-to-one processes in research or safety contexts.
Prior exposure to agentic AI systems and the unique safety challenges introduced by tool use, multi-step reasoning, and autonomous execution.

scope.surface: Safety product layer for North (Cohere's agentic AI platform), bridging safety research and product delivery
primary_metrics_inferred: Safety regression rate before features reach customers
Coverage of safety guardrails across North product surface area
Speed and accuracy of translating research findings into product controls
core_responsibilities: Serve as the product bridge between Cohere's safety research teams and North
Own the safety product roadmap for Cohere and North
Partner with modeling teams to scope and interpret safety evaluations
Define and drive evaluation frameworks for assessing safety properties
Coordinate the development of guardrails and intervention mechanisms
Monitor the AI safety research landscape
Build processes for scaling safety review as North's surface area grows

green_flags: Clearly defined and differentiated role — explicitly states 'This isn't a traditional PM role'
Direct engagement with frontier safety research and modeling teams
Encourages applications from candidates who don't perfectly match all requirements
Strong benefits package including 6 weeks vacation, full health and dental, parental leave top-up
Remote or hybrid flexibility with offices in multiple cities
Equity options mentioned as part of compensation
red_flags: Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed
JD lists hybrid offices as 'San Francisco, New York, and Toronto' under Why Join but location section lists 'New York, Toronto, London, Paris, Zurich' — minor inconsistency in city list
Ambiguity around how much office time is expected for hybrid candidates
Role scope is broad and somewhat undefined, spanning research translation, roadmap ownership, and process-building simultaneously

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years requirement and scope of ownership)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; no mention of direct reports)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from 'Remote or hybrid' language in JD)
ambiguities: Location inconsistency: 'Why Join Cohere' section lists offices as San Francisco, New York, and Toronto; but the Location section states 'Remote or hybrid (New York, Toronto, London, Paris, Zurich)' — San Francisco and Paris appear in company office listings but the role-specific cities are treated as New York, Toronto, London, Paris, Zurich per the Location section.
Work authorization and visa sponsorship are not addressed anywhere in the JD.
Hybrid office time requirement is not specified — 'hybrid' is stated but no percentage or days-per-week is given.
